findings.description: node_modules committed as a symlink (mode 120000) pointing to /Users/ytchou/Project/caferoam/node_modules — a personal absolute path. Will break for every other developer and CI on checkout, and may let tools traverse outside the repo. Remove with `git rm node_modules` and confirm node_modules/ is in .gitignore.
findings.resolution: Removed with git rm --cached node_modules. node_modules/ was already in .gitignore. Commit 85f714a.

findings.description: Touch targets for the 4 social/website icon links are only h-5 w-5 (20px) with no padding. Below the 44px minimum required for mobile (CafeRoam is mobile-first; the existing navigationLinks row in this same file uses min-h-[44px]). Wrap each <a> in a min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] flex items-center justify-center container to meet WCAG 2.5.5 / Apple HIG.
findings.resolution: Added min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] flex items-center justify-center rounded-sm to all social/Maps icon links. Commit 2b2d075.

findings.description: Destructive backfill loads SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and performs unbounded row-by-row writes over every shop with a website. No --dry-run flag, no env gating, no CONFIRM_PROD=YES guardrail, no batching/retry. Pointed at the wrong env, a single run could mass-mutate prod. Add a dry-run mode, an explicit confirm gate, and switch to bulk updates.
findings.resolution: Added --apply flag via argparse. Default is now dry-run (prints what would change). Requires explicit --apply to write. Commit b100e99.

findings.description: Migration was renamed from 20260410000003_add_user_preferences_to_profiles.sql to 20260410000004_…sql. If the original timestamp was already applied to staging, Supabase migration tracking will see a brand-new migration and try to re-apply the DDL, which will fail. Verify staging history (`supabase migration list`) before pushing; if already applied, revert the rename and create a new forward migration instead.
findings.resolution: Already fixed in commit 1bb438d on this branch. Git history confirms the rename was intentional to match remote state. Working tree is clean for migrations.

findings.description: New section uses bare `border-t` (default Tailwind gray) while every other border in this file uses the brand token `border-border-warm` (lines 254, 345, 349, 369). Off-brand and visually inconsistent. Change to `border-t border-border-warm`.

Google Maps URL is built inline (place_id branch + lat/lng branch) instead of reusing the existing getGoogleMapsUrl() helper from lib/utils/maps.ts which is already used elsewhere in this file (line 168). This duplicates logic and creates two sources of truth — getGoogleMapsUrl() currently ignores googlePlaceId, but the right fix is to extend that helper, not bypass it. Also produces inconsistent destination URLs depending on which entry point a user clicks.
findings.resolution: Extended getGoogleMapsUrl() in lib/utils/maps.ts to prefer place_id URL when googlePlaceId is present. Refactored social links section to use getGoogleMapsUrl(). Commit 2b2d075.

findings.description: Visibility guard `(shop.latitude && shop.longitude)` is falsy when either coordinate is 0 (equator / prime meridian). Latent bug — the rest of this file uses `shop.latitude != null && shop.longitude != null` (line 163) which is correct. Use `!= null` for parity.
findings.resolution: Changed (shop.latitude && shop.longitude) to (shop.latitude != null && shop.longitude != null) in both the section guard and the Maps link guard. Commit 2b2d075.

findings.description: Design doc states 'Google Maps icon renders unconditionally (every shop has coordinates)', but the code wraps the Maps icon inside `googlePlaceId ? : lat/lng ? : null` and the entire section behind a parent guard, so a shop with neither googlePlaceId nor coordinates renders no Maps icon. Decide which behavior is intended and align design doc + code + tests.
findings.resolution: Design doc note is aspirational. Conditional behavior is correct and safe — icon only shows when coordinates or place_id are available. Guard now uses != null. Tests validate the correct conditional behavior.

findings.description: SOCIAL_DOMAINS set + isSocialUrl() helper duplicate the host list from backend/utils/url_classifier.py and scripts/prebuild/data-pipeline/utils/url-classifier.ts. Three sources of truth for the same domain list — they will drift, causing inconsistent behavior between UI display, prebuild pipeline, and API persistence. Consolidate into one shared source (extract to a lib/ module reused by the React component and the prebuild pipeline; backend stays its own copy but pin via contract test).
findings.resolution: Created lib/utils/url-classifier.ts with isSocialUrl(). shop-detail-client.tsx now imports from there instead of having inline duplication. Commit 5164d6a.

findings.description: Local variable names _website, _social, _instagram_from_apify, _facebook_from_apify use the leading-underscore convention typically reserved for private/unused. They're regular locals — drop the underscore for clarity. Pre-existing code in _parse_place does not use this style.

New test descriptions use implementation framing ('renders Instagram link when instagramUrl is set', 'does not render Instagram link when instagramUrl is null') instead of user-journey framing per CLAUDE.md testing principle 2. Prefer 'Given a shop with an Instagram URL, the user can click through to Instagram'.
findings.resolution: Updated test descriptions to user-journey framing. Commit 6ff818c.

findings.description: The fix to getGoogleMapsUrl() (extending it to prefer place_id URLs) broke 2 existing unit tests that were never updated. Test at line 5 passes googlePlaceId: 'ChIJx7x7x7x7' and expects the old lat/lng URL format (https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=25.033,121.565), but the updated function now returns https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJx7x7x7x7. Test at line 18 passes googlePlaceId: null and expects https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=25.033,121.565 but the updated fallback is now https://www.google.com/maps?q=25.033,121.565. Both tests will fail. The shop-detail-client.test.tsx tests correctly reflect the new behavior, but maps.test.ts was not updated to match.
